hyperedges.label: Brutalist Pastel Design System cohesion across STYLE.md, DESIGN.md, CLAUDE.md, globals.css
Server-only AI call pattern: gemini lib + API route + client prohibition
TOK data flow: actions.ts mutates tok_exhibitions and tok_objects via RLS

nodes.label: Brand Personality: Focused, Candid, Student-native
nodes.rationale: The product should feel like student-to-student help from someone who understands the IB workload: direct, practical, and specific, without pretending to be an official authority. Avoids generic AI wrapper, corporate edtech dashboard, childish school app, or over-polished SaaS framing.

nodes.label: Design Principles
nodes.rationale: Five principles: make next academic move obvious; feedback must be specific enough to act on; keep student in control (AI supports thinking, not submission-ready output); respect IB seriousness while staying student-native; prefer structured workflows over open-ended chat.

nodes.label: WCAG 2.1 AA Accessibility Baseline
nodes.rationale: Target WCAG 2.1 AA. Keyboard access for all controls, visible focus states, readable contrast, predictable layouts. Reduced-motion-friendly interactions especially for TOK prompt tour. Avoid relying on color alone for meaning.

nodes.label: Brutalist Pastel Design System
nodes.rationale: No shadows, gradients, blur effects, or rounded pill shapes. 2px solid black borders everywhere, 4px radius, warm cream background, system font stack. Pastel accents (yellow, pink, mint, sky) for tags, highlights, and callouts only.

nodes.label: Creative North Star: The Marked-Up Study Desk
nodes.rationale: Interface should feel like a focused study desk with useful annotations in the margins. Direct, tactile, and a little handmade but serious enough for real IB work. Supports the student trying to make progress now, not perform productivity.
nodes.norm_label: creative north star: the marked-up study desk

nodes.label: The Highlighter Rule
nodes.rationale: Pastels should mark categories, availability, and key student actions. Do not wash whole screens in pastel.

nodes.label: The Ink Rule
nodes.rationale: Borders are part of the identity. Use the same ink color and 2px stroke consistently rather than mixing gray outlines.

nodes.label: The Flat-Until-Action Rule
nodes.rationale: Surfaces stay flat until interaction. Card bump shadow (8px 8px 0 0) only on hover for clickable cards. Do not add ambient dashboard shadows or floating glass panels.

nodes.label: The No-Display-UI Rule
nodes.rationale: Product controls, labels, and data should use the system sans. Save hero-scale type for true page heroes.

nodes.label: Design Anti-patterns (glassmorphism, gradient text, ambient shadow)
nodes.rationale: Explicitly forbidden: glassmorphism, gradient text, decorative blur, ambient shadow systems, em dashes or double hyphens in copy, side-stripe border accents, color-only state encoding for TOK categories.
nodes.norm_label: design anti-patterns (glassmorphism, gradient text, ambient shadow)

nodes.label: Next.js Agent Rules (breaking changes warning)
nodes.rationale: Explicitly warns agents: this is NOT the Next.js from training data. Breaking changes apply. Must read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing code and heed deprecation notices.

nodes.label: Next.js 16 Breaking Changes
nodes.rationale: middleware.ts renamed to proxy.ts; export function must be named proxy not middleware; runtime config option not available in proxy files.

nodes.label: Request Flow: browser → proxy.ts → app/ → Supabase/AI
nodes.rationale: All authenticated requests go through proxy.ts which gates /dashboard/** and /profile/** — unauthenticated redirected to /login, authenticated away from /login.
nodes.norm_label: request flow: browser → proxy.ts → app/ → supabase/ai

nodes.label: Supabase Two-Client Pattern
nodes.rationale: Browser client (createBrowserClient) must be instantiated inside event handlers, never at module scope — module-level call breaks SSR prerender. Server client (createServerClient) with cookie wiring for RSC and Route Handlers. Never mix the two.

nodes.label: AI Calls Server-Only Pattern
nodes.rationale: All Gemini calls go through app/api/ai/route.ts. Client components never import lib/gemini.ts directly. Endpoint accepts {prompt, systemPrompt?}, returns {text}. Justification saves through separate route that verifies ownership.

nodes.label: Row Level Security (RLS) via auth.uid()
nodes.rationale: RLS enabled on all tables. Policies are user-scoped via auth.uid(). Migrations run manually in Supabase SQL Editor, never via CLI.

hyperedges.label: Brutalist Pastel Design System cohesion across STYLE.md, DESIGN.md, CLAUDE.md, globals.css
Server-only AI call pattern: gemini lib + API route + client prohibition
